data.body:
Pre-covid, I left for work early to get a coffee at my local coffee shop. A group of us met every morning, to get coffees and chat before leaving for our respective jobs. We're still friends.

It was the best way to start my day. It makes me sad how anti-social our world is getting set up to become.

The fun part is *any* potential energy minimum (fancy word for basically anything that goes back and forth) can be approximated by that!

data.body:
From out here, with some irony, the story reminds me a lot of Gary, Indiana. 

And there's also some deep trauma in the misery of the coal industry coupled with the economic prosperity it brought with it. I didn't grow up in it but I can feel it.
